name: Amazon SageMaker
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and governance adoption pack for SageMaker endpoints and ML workflows.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track endpoint invocation volume, latency, errors, model/container health, and resource utilization.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Use Clarify, feature attribution, model cards, and lineage to explain model behavior.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure prediction quality, data drift, model drift, baseline deviation, and endpoint SLOs.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Validate bias, data protection, endpoint access, encryption, and model governance controls.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously validate model performance, drift, retraining signals, and deployment safety.

sections.name: Endpoint Overview
sections.purpose: Endpoint status, variant traffic, invocation volume, and deployment state.

sections.name: Latency and Errors
sections.purpose: Model latency, overhead latency, invocation errors, 4xx/5xx, and timeout behavior.

sections.name: Resource Utilization
sections.purpose: CPU, memory, GPU, disk, and instance-level pressure for hosted endpoints.

sections.name: Data and Model Quality
sections.purpose: Model Monitor baselines, data drift, constraint violations, and missing features.

sections.name: Explainability and Bias
sections.purpose: Clarify bias reports, feature attribution, and model card evidence.

sections.name: Security Posture
sections.purpose: Endpoint IAM access, encryption, network isolation, CloudTrail changes, and Security Hub findings.

metrics.name: Invocation4XXErrors
metrics.why: Detect client request or validation issues.

metrics.name: Invocation5XXErrors
metrics.why: Detect model container or service-side failures.

metrics.name: OverheadLatency
metrics.why: Measure SageMaker overhead outside model execution.

log_queries.name: SageMaker endpoint errors
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /(?i)(error|exception|traceback|modelerror|timeout)/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: SageMaker slow inference
log_queries.intent: Find slow model invocation log entries when latency is logged.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message, latencyMs | filter latencyMs > 1000 | sort latencyMs desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: SageMaker endpoint changes
log_queries.intent: Find endpoint configuration and deployment changes in CloudTrail logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.arn, requestParameters.endpointName | filter eventSource = "sagemaker.amazonaws.com" and eventName like /CreateEndpoint|UpdateEndpoint|DeleteEndpoint|CreateEndpointConfig/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

alarms.name: SageMaker5xxHigh
alarms.action: Inspect endpoint logs, container health, model artifact, and recent deployments.

alarms.name: SageMakerLatencyHigh
alarms.condition: ModelLatency or OverheadLatency above SLO threshold
alarms.action: Review model performance, instance size, autoscaling, and payload size.

alarms.name: SageMakerInvocationDrop
alarms.action: Check upstream callers, endpoint health, DNS, and deployment changes.

alarms.name: SageMakerModelMonitorViolation
alarms.action: Review drift report, input data quality, and retraining workflow.

security_controls.name: Endpoint IAM least privilege
security_controls.evidence: InvokeEndpoint permissions are scoped to approved applications and endpoints.

security_controls.name: Encryption
security_controls.evidence: Model artifacts, data capture, and endpoint resources use approved encryption settings.

security_controls.name: Network isolation
security_controls.evidence: Endpoints use VPC configuration or approved network controls for private workloads.

security_controls.name: Model quality monitoring
security_controls.evidence: Model Monitor baselines and schedules exist for production models where required.

security_controls.name: Explainability and bias review
security_controls.evidence: Clarify reports or model cards are maintained for governed ML use cases.

adoption_plan: Classify SageMaker endpoints by business criticality and model risk.
Enable endpoint logs, CloudWatch metrics, and data capture where required.
Create dashboards for latency, errors, invocations, resource pressure, and drift.
Create alarms for 5xx, latency, traffic drop, and Model Monitor violations.
Review endpoint IAM, encryption, VPC configuration, and artifact access.
Attach Model Monitor and Clarify evidence for governed models.
Promote the pack as the default SageMaker production governance standard.
